map.alt: Blank outline map of Europe and Asia

items.label: Roman Empire
items.hint: Centered on Rome, it ringed the entire Mediterranean at its height.

items.label: Byzantine Empire
items.hint: Rome's eastern half outlived the west by a thousand years, ruled from Constantinople.

items.label: Persian Empire
items.hint: The Achaemenid capital of Persepolis lay in modern-day Iran.

items.label: Maurya Empire
items.hint: Under Ashoka, this empire united most of the Indian subcontinent.

items.label: Han Dynasty
items.hint: Ruled from Chang'an, contemporaneous with Rome at the other end of the Silk Road.

items.label: Mongol Empire
items.hint: The largest contiguous land empire in history, ruled from Karakorum in Mongolia.
